role: The complete source code implementation of the ArchSpine semantic mirror system.
responsibility: Provides the entire codebase for the ArchSpine system, encompassing asset templates for documentation standards, AST parsing and language discovery, command-line interface, core L2 aggregation infrastructure, architectural rule enforcement engines, foundational infrastructure services (config, storage, LLM, MCP), service orchestration for check/fix/sync commands, pipeline task stages, type definitions and configuration schemas, and utility modules for file synchronization and system operations. Collectively, these components implement the full lifecycle of architectural analysis, validation, repair, and documentation generation for both human and AI consumption.

children.role: This directory contains the core template definitions and documentation standards for the ArchSpine mirror system.
AST parsing and language discovery layer for the ArchSpine mirror system.

The command-line interface layer of the ArchSpine semantic mirror system, providing the main entry point, command routing, help system, initialization logic, repository artifact management, and utility functions for CLI operations.
This directory provides the core L2 aggregation infrastructure for the ArchSpine mirror system.
Core engine module for the ArchSpine mirror system, providing file scanning, architectural rule enforcement, context resolution, and fix generation capabilities.
Infrastructure layer providing foundational services and abstractions for the ArchSpine mirror system.
Service layer facade and orchestration for ArchSpine's core commands (check, fix, sync) and runtime management.
Pipeline stage tasks that orchestrate the core processing phases of the ArchSpine mirror system, from scanning and extraction to summarization, validation, and view derivation.
Foundational type definitions and configuration schema for the ArchSpine mirror system.

Core utility modules supporting file synchronization, CLI presentation, user interaction, cryptographic footprints, atomic file operations, Git hook management, process locking, and path normalization for the ArchSpine system.
